name: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507
note: Paired always-on reasoning ('Thinking-only') sibling of Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507, released 4 days later. Enforces <think> mode, extends max output to 81,920 tokens, native 262,144-token context. Same 235B/22B-active MoE backbone as the Instruct-2507 release already in corpus, but a distinct fine-tune (not a shared-weights effort tier), so tracked as its own standalone entry.

results.notes: Listed as 'CFEval' in the official model card's Performance table (Coding section). Elo-style rating, not a 0-100 score; same convention already used elsewhere in corpus (e.g. Qwen3.5-122B-A10B) for Qwen's internal Codeforces-style eval. Treat as vendor-reported, not necessarily apples-to-apples with third-party CodeForces evals.

LMArena (arena.ai) live text-arena Elo, listed exactly as 'qwen3-235b-a22b-thinking-2507' (rank 127, score 1399±7 as of this July 2026 snapshot) -- confirmed this is the plain text model, not the separate 'Qwen3 VL 235B A22B Thinking' vision-language variant (score 1396, rank 59) that also appears on the same leaderboard, to avoid conflating the two distinct models. New slug for this model: file had arena-hard-v2 (self-reported) but not plain 'arena' (independent crowdsourced Elo).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real and released via direct fetch of the HF model card and independently via Simon Willison's July 25, 2025 blog post and OpenRouter listing. Full benchmark table transcribed verbatim from the HF model card (MMLU-Pro 84.4, MMLU-Redux 93.8, GPQA 81.1, SuperGPQA 64.9, AIME25 92.3, HLE 18.2, LiveCodeBench v6 74.1, IFEval 87.8, Arena-Hard v2 79.7, BFCL-v3 71.9, TAU2-Telecom 45.6, among others). Pricing ($0.23/$2.30 per M tokens, Intl/Singapore tier) confirmed exactly via Alibaba Cloud Model Studio's official pricing page, which lists the model by its exact HF name. langCount (119) carried over from the Qwen3 Technical Report figure already used for the sibling Instruct-2507 entry in corpus, since both are the same base model family/training corpus.

2026-07-16 completeness check for the five missing core slugs (mmlu, math, humaneval, swe-bench, mmmu), all resolving to omitted_no_evidence: (1) mmlu -- the official HF model card's full Performance table (transcribed verbatim: MMLU-Pro 84.4, MMLU-Redux 93.8, GPQA 81.1, SuperGPQA 64.9, AIME25 92.3, HMMT25 83.9, LiveBench 78.4, HLE 18.2, LiveCodeBench v6 74.1, CFEval 2134, OJBench 32.5, IFEval 87.8, Arena-Hard v2 79.7, Creative Writing v3 86.1, WritingBench 88.3, BFCL-v3 71.9, TAU1/TAU2-Retail/Airline/Telecom, MultiIF 80.6, MMLU-ProX 81.0, INCLUDE 81.0, PolyMATH 60.1) has no raw MMLU row -- only MMLU-Pro and MMLU-Redux, both already tracked under their own slugs. No independent leaderboard (Epoch AI, Artificial Analysis, llm-stats.com) surfaces a raw-MMLU number for this checkpoint either. (2) math -- not in the 2507 model card's table at all (Qwen replaced legacy MATH-500 with AIME25/HMMT25/PolyMATH for this release, a deliberate harder-benchmark swap). The sibling original Thinking checkpoint (April 2025, non-2507) does report MATH-500=98 self-reported via the Qwen3 Technical Report Table 11, but that source predates the 2507 refresh by ~2 months and covers a different checkpoint, so it cannot be attributed here. (3) humaneval -- absent from the HF card, Epoch AI, Vals.ai (model not listed there under this exact name), Artificial Analysis, and llm-stats.com; HumanEval is a saturated legacy benchmark Qwen has dropped in favor of LiveCodeBench/CFEval/OJBench for this release. (4) swe-bench -- the only SWE-bench-adjacent number found is Scale AI's SWE-Bench Pro public leaderboard entry labeled plainly 'qwen3-235b-a22b' (21.41±2.25); confirmed via direct fetch of labs.scale.com/leaderboard/swe_bench_pro_public that there is no distinct '-thinking-2507' row, and this same ambiguous entry is already attributed to the sibling original (April 2025) Thinking checkpoint's corpus file, so it cannot be safely re-attributed to the 2507 checkpoint without risking a duplicate/mislabeled claim. (5) mmmu -- this model is text-only (modalities: [\"text\"], capabilities.vision: false, confirmed on both the HF card and OpenRouter listing); MMMU requires image inputs, so its absence is structurally expected, not a reporting gap -- Qwen's multimodal MMMU evaluations live under the separate Qwen3-VL family, a distinct corpus entry. Checked in order: official HF model card (full table transcribed), independent leaderboards (Epoch AI model page + search index, Vals.ai model index, Artificial Analysis model page, llm-stats.com, Scale AI SWE-Bench Pro leaderboard), and sibling cross-reference (Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 and Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking [April 2025] corpus files).

2026-07-18 targeted re-chase of the two current-gen gaps flagged as highest-priority (swe-bench, mmmu), going beyond the 2026-07-16 sweep with additional direct-fetch verification (not just search-engine summaries, which proved unreliable -- see below):

SWE-BENCH (still omitted_no_evidence, now checked across all 6 prescribed methods with direct fetches):
1. Official model card: re-fetched the raw HF README.md directly (not just the rendered page) and grepped case-insensitively for "swe" across all 378 lines -- zero genuine matches (the full Performance table, already transcribed verbatim in the prior note, has no SWE-bench row).
2. Independent trackers: (a) official SWE-bench.com results viewer (swebench.com/viewer.html) fetched directly and grepped for every "Qwen" mention in the raw HTML -- the only Qwen entries present anywhere on the leaderboard are Qwen2.5-72B, Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct, Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct, and Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct; no "Thinking" variant of any Qwen3 model appears at all. (b) llm-stats.com's dedicated SWE-bench Verified leaderboard page fetched directly (full raw HTML, not the truncated top-50 AI summary) and grepped for "qwen3-235b" / "thinking-2507" -- zero matches; the only Qwen entries present are later-generation models (Qwen3.5/3.6/3.7 series), confirming this July-2025 checkpoint was never submitted to that tracker. (c) Artificial Analysis model page returned HTTP 404 for this exact model slug. (d) Scale AI's SEAL Showdown is a preference/chat arena, not a SWE-bench evaluation, so it is not a valid source for this benchmark regardless of ranking mentioned in search summaries.
3. Academic sources: a web-search AI summary initially claimed the GLM-4.5 technical report (arXiv:2508.06471) contains a SWE-bench Verified score for "Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507" -- this was verified against the actual paper text (fetched via ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org) and found to be a hallucination/misattribution: GLM-4.5's Table 5 SWE-bench Verified comparison (GLM-4.5 64.2%, o3 69.1%, Claude Opus 4 67.8%, Claude Sonnet 4 70.4%, Gemini 2.5 Pro 49.0%, DeepSeek R1 41.4%, Kimi K2 65.4%, GPT-4.1 48.6%) does not include any Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking model. No other arXiv paper was found with a genuine SWE-bench Verified number attributed to this exact checkpoint.
4. GitHub eval-harness logs: found nebius/SWE-rebench-openhands-Qwen3-235B-A22B (HF) and an associated Nebius blog post reporting 61.7% Pass@1 on SWE-bench Verified with the OpenHands scaffold -- but this is a distinct fine-tuned derivative trained on OpenHands trajectories (a separate weights release built from the Instruct-2507 base), not this corpus entry's Thinking-2507 checkpoint, so it cannot be attributed here without mislabeling a different model's result.
5. Sibling-model comparison: cross-checked the sibling Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 corpus file directly -- it also has no swe-bench entry, confirming the whole July-2025 2507 release wave simply did not report SWE-bench Verified (the family's SWE-bench-reporting releases only start with Qwen3-Coder and later 3.5/3.6/3.7-series models).
6. SWE-rebench (swe-rebench.com, run by Nebius) does list "Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507" as a model card in its leaderboard history/changelog -- but SWE-rebench is a distinct, separate continuously-updated contamination-resistant benchmark from SWE-bench Verified (this corpus's 'swe-bench' slug is specifically "SWE-bench Verified" per corpus/benchmarks.json), so a SWE-rebench score cannot be substituted for the swe-bench slug without conflating two different benchmarks; the full leaderboard table itself could not be fetched (page exceeds fetch size limits) so no clean SWE-rebench number was even confirmed either way.
Conclusion: genuine absence confirmed across official card, both official and third-party SWE-bench Verified leaderboards (direct HTML fetch, not just search summaries), academic literature (with one AI-summary hallucination caught and corrected via primary-source verification), GitHub artifacts (found only for a differently-branded derivative model), and sibling cross-reference. Remains omitted_no_evidence.

MMMU (reconfirmed omitted_no_evidence / architecturally inapplicable):
Re-verified text-only status directly from the raw HF README.md (fetched independently of the rendered page) -- zero "mmmu" mentions anywhere in the document, and the only "image" reference in the entire file is the model's banner JPEG (a document image, not a vision-input capability demo). This corroborates the corpus file's own modalities:["text"] and capabilities.vision:false fields, and the 2026-07-16 note's finding that Qwen's multimodal/MMMU evaluations live under the separate Qwen3-VL family. No further search was needed per the task's own instruction that a text-only model makes MMMU structurally inapplicable rather than an unpublished-data gap.
